process_name: Long-lived neutral-kaon rare semileptonic muon mode
implementation_difficulty_reason: Requires a new Delta S = 1 semileptonic rare-kaon observable, short-distance s -> d mu+mu- matching, indirect/direct CP separation with interference, and long-distance two-photon/CPC inputs. Existing Delta F=2 SLL/SLR/VLL/VRR/LR machinery does not cover this mode.

grep_commands: rg -n -F -e 'K0L --> pi0 mu+ mu-' -e 'K_L -> pi0 mu' -e 'K_L \\to \\pi^0\\mu' -e 'pi0 mu+ mu-' -e 'S013R30' -e 'S013.16' -e 'S012R16' -e 'a_S' -e 'C_mix' -e 's -> d mu' quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "epsilon_K|epsilon_k|Delta m_K|DELTA_M_K|evaluate_delta_mk|B_d|b_d|B_s|b_s|D0|d_mix|mu.?e.?gamma|muToEGamma|BR_LIMIT|MEG" quarkConstraints flavorConstraints scanParams neutrinos yukawa qcd scripts tests docs -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "MODERN_PHENOMENOLOGY_SYSTEM_IDS|DEFAULT_DELTA_F2_INPUTS|evaluate_bd_mixing|evaluate_bs_mixing|evaluate_d0_mixing|check_mu_to_e_gamma" quarkConstraints flavorConstraints scanParams tests docs -g '!*.ipynb'
code_coverage.result: No K_L -> pi0 mu+ mu-, K_S -> pi0 mu+ mu-, a_S, or s -> d mu+mu- implementation found in the requested code directories.
nearest_existing_coverage: quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:209: DEFAULT_DELTA_F2_INPUTS_V1 starts the existing Delta F=2 observable list.

post_2010_developments.relation: Exploratory lattice-QCD method for long-distance K -> pi l+ l- amplitudes.
Snowmass rare-kaon white paper emphasizing K_{L,S} -> pi0 l+ l- branching ratios and spectra.

status_history.reason: initial PKA source extraction and draft
Writer polish complete for batch wa_w5b_kaon.

issues: CHK-1: Several numerical claims in K009.tex are source-backed in supporting_experimental_values or theory_decomposition, but not represented as YAML pdg_or_equivalent entries with full year/value/uncertainty/source_url/access_date/sha256 metadata. Examples include the KTeV two-candidate and 0.87 +/- 0.15 background claim, the supporting K_S -> pi0 mu+ mu- 2.9e-9 value and 0.22 +0.18 -0.11 background claim, and the Isidori-Smith-Unterdorfer coefficient and SM-expectation block.

status_history.reason: Cycle 2 metadata rework: promoted CA-listed numerical claims into pdg_or_equivalent values.
Cycle 3 checker re-check passed CHK-1 through CHK-8 after WA-v2 metadata rework.
Opus round 2 per-process sign-off; CA-verified PASS retained; see flavor_catalog/signoff/round_002_index.md.
Independent live-source fact-check completed for kaon family.
status_history.notes: Live source fetch and local snapshot checks verified all value claims for this process.

sections.process:
\textbf{Standard notation:} \(K_L \to \pi^0\mu^+\mu^-\).  This entry covers the
long-lived neutral-kaon rare semileptonic muon channel, a \(\Delta S=1\)
weak-neutral-current process receiving indirect-CP, direct-CP, interference,
and CP-conserving two-photon contributions.
sections.pdg_prose:
The canonical measured limit is
\[
  {\cal B}(K_L \to \pi^0\mu^+\mu^-) < 3.8\times10^{-10}
  \quad(90\%\ {\rm CL}),
\]
from the 2026 PDG pdgLive entry \(S013.16/S013R30\), accessed 2026-05-16
(\texttt{pdg2026\_kl\_pi0mumu\_pdgLive.txt}).  PDG traces the active limit to
the KTeV search in PRL 84, 5279.  The KTeV snapshot records two observed
events, \(0.87\pm0.15\) expected background events, and the same
\(3.8\times10^{-10}\) upper limit
(\texttt{ktev2000\_kl\_pi0mumu\_arxiv.txt}).
sections.relevance:
This mode probes \(s\to d\mu^+\mu^-\) semileptonic and electroweak-penguin
structures, not the \(\Delta F=2\) KK-gluon mixing lane currently implemented.
In RS or anarchic-flavor variants it is sensitive to flavor-changing \(Z\)
couplings, heavy neutral gauge exchange, and short-distance vector/axial
operators.  The muon mode's CP-conserving two-photon contribution is
numerically important in the PKA theory decomposition, so a future signal would
require separating CPV short-distance information from long-distance hadronic
input.
sections.post_2008:
No post-2008 experimental result was found that supersedes the KTeV/PDG
\(K_L\to\pi^0\mu^+\mu^-\) limit.  The supporting short-lived mode remains the
NA48/PDG \(K_S\to\pi^0\mu^+\mu^-\) value
\((2.9^{+1.5}_{-1.2}\pm0.2)\times10^{-9}\), based on six observed events and
\(0.22^{+0.18}_{-0.11}\) expected background events
(\texttt{na48\_ks\_pi0mumu\_pdgLive.txt},
\texttt{na48\_ks\_pi0mumu\_arxiv.txt}).  Since arXiv:0804.1954, the main
developments are interpretive: exploratory lattice work has started on
long-distance \(K\to\pi\ell^+\ell^-\) amplitudes
(\texttt{christ\_feng\_juettner\_lawson\_portelli\_sachrajda2016\_lattice\_arxiv.txt}),
and the Snowmass rare-kaon white paper keeps
\(K_{L,S}\to\pi^0\ell^+\ell^-\) branching ratios and spectra in the
short-distance rare-kaon program
(\texttt{aebischer\_buras\_kumar2022\_rare\_kaon\_arxiv.txt}).
sections.validity:
Class: long-distance-limited and CP-decomposition-dependent.  The
Isidori--Smith--Unterdorfer decomposition writes the rate as mixing,
interference, direct, and CP-conserving pieces multiplying \(10^{-12}\).  For
the muon mode the coefficients are
\[
 C_{\rm mix}^{\mu}=(3.7\pm0.1)|a_S|^2,\quad
 C_{\rm int}^{\mu}=(1.6\pm0.1)|a_S|,\quad
 C_{\rm dir}^{\mu}=1.0\pm0.1,\quad
 C_{\rm CPC}^{\mu}=5.2\pm1.6,
\]
with \(|a_S|=1.2\pm0.2\).  The same paper quotes the constructive-interference
SM expectation as \((1.5\pm0.3)\times10^{-11}\), well below the current
experimental limit.  These numerical theory inputs are recorded in the YAML
\texttt{theory\_decomposition} block and trace to
\texttt{isidori\_smith\_unterdorfer2004\_kl\_pi0mumu\_arxiv.txt}.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
\textbf{NO.}  Targeted greps over \texttt{quarkConstraints/}, \texttt{qcd/},
\texttt{flavorConstraints/}, \texttt{neutrinos/}, \texttt{yukawa/},
\texttt{warpConfig/}, \texttt{solvers/}, \texttt{scanParams/}, and
\texttt{tests/} found no \(K_L\to\pi^0\mu^+\mu^-\), \(K_S\to\pi^0\mu^+\mu^-\),
\(a_S\), or \(s\to d\mu^+\mu^-\) implementation.  Existing nearby kaon coverage
is \(\Delta F=2\), e.g. \texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:209} and the modern
policy list at \texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23}.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
\textbf{HIGH.}  A production constraint needs a new \(\Delta S=1\) semileptonic
observable, short-distance \(s\to d\mu^+\mu^-\) matching, CPV/interference
bookkeeping tied to \(K_S\to\pi^0\mu^+\mu^-\), and a long-distance
two-photon/CPC treatment.  The existing \(\Delta F=2\) SLL/SLR/VLL/VRR/LR
basis is not sufficient.
sections.key_references:
Process-local source keys before bibliography consolidation:
\texttt{PDG2026:K009}, \texttt{KTeV2000:KLPi0MuMu},
\texttt{NA482004:KSPi0MuMu},
\texttt{IsidoriSmithUnterdorfer2004:KLPi0MuMu},
\texttt{CFW2008:RSFlavor}, \texttt{ChristEtAl2016:KPiLLLattice}, and
\texttt{AebischerBurasKumar2022:RareKaons}.

priority_rationale_short: Muon companion rare mode, weaker and long-distance limited
priority_rationale_full:
K_L -> pi0 mu+ mu- tests the same broad s -> d neutral-current sector as the
electron mode, with sensitivity to flavor-changing Z and heavy neutral-gauge
exchange. Its current upper limit is weaker relative to the expected
short-distance scale, and the CP-conserving two-photon piece is numerically
important. It is therefore a useful companion observable but not a major
driver of the anarchic RS scan.
